Dimensions of Ring-Joint Facings
Dimensions

Table 5
Table 5 is a table listing dimensions of Ring-Joint Facings for various pressure rating classes.

Pressure–Temperature Ratings
Pressure–Temperature Ratings are technical specifications that define the maximum allowable working pressures for materials at different service temperatures, particularly in the context of pipe flanges and fittings. These ratings establish the operational limits of components under varying thermal and pressure conditions, ensuring safe and effective performance in industrial or engineering applications. They are typically presented in tabular formats, detailing pressure thresholds for specific materials across multiple temperature classes or ranges. The data is material-specific and often included in a Mandatory Appendix of relevant standards, providing critical information for selecting appropriate components based on their intended use. The tables may express values in both metric (bar/Celsius) and imperial (psi/Fahrenheit) units to accommodate diverse engineering requirements. Pressure–Temperature Ratings are essential for determining the suitability of materials and components in systems where temperature fluctuations could affect pressure integrity, such as in piping networks or mechanical equipment subjected to thermal stress.

Bolt Circle or Bolt Spacing
Bolt Circle or Bolt Spacing and Diameter of Drilled Holes are related as specifications for flanged fittings in Table 9.

B16 Standards Committee
The B16 Standards Committee is affiliated with The American Society of Mechanical Engineers." But the correct entity name for the committee is "ASME B16 Committee", so the source entity should be "ASME B16 Committee" and the target is "The American Society of Mechanical Engineers".

Market Selloff
The decline in the Global Tech Index is part of the broader market selloff driven by investor concerns." So in this case, the pressure-temperature ratings (as data) are related to the groups (concepts).<SEP>The decline in the Global Tech Index is part of the broader market selloff driven by investor concerns." So they linked the index to the selloff. In this case, the pressure ratings are data points, so the relationship between the material and the temperature would have the pressure as a keyword. But the previous output might have missed some of these relationships. For example, in the first table, "25Cr–12Ni" is associated with "A351 Gr. CH8" and "A351 Gr. CH20". The previous extraction didn't include these relationships. So I need to add those.

Ring Joint Facing Dimensions
Table II-5 provides ring joint facing dimensions for the fittings." That seems correct.

Note (7)

A240 Gr. 347H
A240 Gr. 347H is restricted from use above 538°C due to safety or performance concerns." was included. But the note also mentions that for temperatures over 538°C, the material must be heat treated. So there's a relationship between the material and the heat treatment method, and another between the heat treatment and the temperature. The initial extraction might have missed the heat treatment method's relationship with the temperature.

Service Temperatures
Service temperatures above 538°C require materials to meet ASTM 6 grain size specifications for safe application.

Group 1.18 Materials
The method of normalized and tempered material is applied to Group 1.18 Materials, with limitations on usage above certain temperatures." That's accurate.
